verdicts.reasoning: The scanner flagged array_map(self::class . '::clean_input', $value) at class-joinchat-util.php:51 as RCE. This is a recursive self-call to Joinchat_Util::clean_input() — a hardcoded callback, not user-controlled. The function applies wp_unslash, sanitize_text_field, and trim. array_map with a hardcoded callback is not RCE. The 'self::class' expression resolves at compile time to 'Joinchat_Util'. No user input controls the callback name.

The scanner flagged array_map('trim', ...) at class-joinchat-util.php:566 as RCE. The callback is the hardcoded string 'trim' — not user-controlled. The function get_client_ip() reads $_SERVER headers and passes IPs through array_map('trim', ...) followed by filter_var(FILTER_VALIDATE_IP). This is standard IP parsing, not command execution. The 'trim' callback cannot execute arbitrary code.

The scanner flagged array_filter() at class-joinchat-tracking.php:360 as RCE. The array_filter callback is an inline anonymous function that compares timestamps with (int) cast. The data source is get_option() — WordPress options table — which requires admin access to modify. array_filter with a closure that does integer arithmetic is not RCE. The stored data keys are SHA-256 hashes (line 343), values are integer timestamps.

The scanner flagged wp_remote_post() at class-joinchat-admin-onboard.php:243 as SSRF. The URL is hardcoded to 'https://eu5-api.connectif.cloud:443/...' — user input does NOT control the destination URL. The user-controlled data ($data['newsletter']) is sent as the POST body (email field), not as the URL. Additionally, this endpoint is behind wp_ajax_joinchat_n (admin-only AJAX) with check_ajax_referer('joinchat_onboard') nonce verification. An attacker cannot redirect the request to an internal service.

The scanner flagged echo apply_filters('joinchat_field_output', $output, ...) at class-joinchat-admin-page.php:650 as XSS. The $output is constructed entirely from: (1) esc_html__() translated strings, (2) absint($delay) and (int)$pages for numeric values, (3) checked() for checkbox state, (4) esc_url_raw(admin_url(...)) for URLs, (5) $badge which comes from jc_common()->settings stored by admin. All dynamic values are properly escaped. This is an admin-only settings page requiring manage_options capability. Even the apply_filters wrapper is a WordPress standard pattern for extensibility — the base output is safe.

The scanner flagged printf($link, esc_attr($tab), esc_html($name)) at class-joinchat-admin-page.php:840 as XSS. The $active_tab comes from $_GET['tab'] but is validated with in_array(..., array_keys($this->tabs), true) using strict comparison. If the value is not in the whitelist, it defaults to 'general'. The $link template is one of two hardcoded strings selected based on whether $active_tab === $tab. The printf output uses esc_attr($tab) and esc_html($name) where $tab and $name come from the $this->tabs whitelist. This is properly sanitized. Additionally, this is an admin-only page.

The scanner flagged echo apply_filters('joinchat_metabox_output', ...) at class-joinchat-admin.php:387 as XSS from post_meta. The $metadata comes from get_post_meta() and is rendered in admin/partials/post-meta-box.php. Examining that template: telephone uses esc_attr($metadata['telephone']), message_text uses esc_textarea($metadata['message_text']), message_send uses esc_textarea($metadata['message_send']), and view uses checked() which only outputs 'checked' or empty. All values are properly escaped. The meta_box function is only called in the admin post editor (add_meta_box context).

The scanner flagged Joinchat_Util::clean_input($metadata) at class-joinchat-admin.php:423 as RCE. clean_input() recursively calls sanitize_text_field() on string values and array_map(self::class.'::clean_input') on arrays — both hardcoded callbacks. The save_meta() function at line 399 checks wp_verify_nonce('joinchat_data') and is hooked to save_post/edited_term (admin actions). The $_POST data is sanitized through clean_input before being stored. array_filter() on line 423 uses default behavior (removes falsy values). No RCE vector exists.

The scanner flagged echo apply_filters('joinchat_term_metabox_output', ...) at class-joinchat-admin.php:499 as XSS from term_meta. The $metadata from get_term_meta() is rendered in admin/partials/term-meta-box.php. That template uses esc_attr($metadata['telephone']), esc_textarea($metadata['message_text']), esc_textarea($metadata['message_send']), and checked() for the view field. All dynamic values are properly escaped. This is admin-only (term edit screen).

The scanner flagged preg_replace('/^\{\s*\}$/', '', $settings['message_text']) at class-joinchat-public.php:75 as RCE. In modern PHP (7+), the /e modifier for preg_replace is removed and throws an error. This regex does NOT use the /e modifier — it simply replaces the literal pattern '{  }' (curly braces with optional whitespace) with an empty string. preg_replace without /e is a string operation, not code execution. The data comes from term/post meta set by admins, and is later passed through esc_html() in formatted_message() at class-joinchat-util.php:257 before output.

Identical analysis to rce-005. The scanner flagged preg_replace('/^\{\s*\}$/', '', $settings['message_send']) at class-joinchat-public.php:76. Same pattern without /e modifier — pure string replacement. The message_send value originates from admin-set post/term meta, sanitized through clean_input() on save, and the preg_replace is a benign whitespace-matching regex. No code execution path exists.

summary: All 11 scanner findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly classified standard PHP functions (array_map with hardcoded callbacks, array_filter with closures, preg_replace without /e modifier) as RCE sinks. XSS findings were flagged on admin-only pages where all dynamic values use proper WordPress escaping (esc_attr, esc_html, esc_textarea, absint). The SSRF finding targeted a hardcoded URL where user input only affects the POST body, not the destination. The plugin demonstrates solid security practices: nonce verification on all form handlers, sanitize_text_field on inputs, proper escaping on outputs, and admin capability checks on all settings pages.
feedback: Scanner has extremely high false positive rate on this plugin (100%). Key issues: (1) array_map/array_filter/call_user_func with hardcoded callbacks should not be flagged as RCE — the callback must be user-controlled for exploitation. (2) preg_replace without the /e modifier is not an RCE sink. (3) Scanner should check WordPress auth context (admin_menu, wp_ajax_ vs wp_ajax_nopriv_) before marking findings as 'auth_required: false'. (4) Scanner should recognize WordPress escaping functions (esc_attr, esc_html, esc_textarea, absint, checked) as sanitizers.
